title: Media detail page
analysis:
Task 44 builds the media detail view at /media/{id} with HTMX-powered tabbed UI displaying metadata, transcript, detections, faces, audio events, and embeddings. The existing codebase provides: (1) CatalogDB with all needed query methods: get_media(), get_transcript(), get_detections_for_media(), get_faces_for_media(), get_audio_events_for_media(), get_embeddings_for_media(), get_face_clusters(), get_face_cluster_by_id(). (2) media.py routes with /media list page and /api/media JSON endpoint following _get_db(request) pattern. (3) Jinja2 templates extending base.html with Tailwind CSS dark theme + HTMX. (4) Test patterns in test_media_list.py showing seeded DB fixtures and TestClient testing for both JSON API and HTML endpoints.

Approach: Add new routes to media.py for the detail page + API endpoints. CatalogDB already has all needed per-table query methods; add a get_media_detail() convenience aggregator. Templates use HTMX tab switching: clicking a tab loads a partial via hx-get to /media/{id}/tab/{name}. The detail.html page renders metadata tab by default. Each tab is a separate Jinja2 partial. Three JSON API endpoints (/api/media/{id}, /api/media/{id}/transcript, /api/media/{id}/detections) serve programmatic access. The media_row.html partial gets updated to link filenames to the detail page. Detection/transcript/audio data is parsed from JSON in Python, not templates.

prerequisites.description: Create test file tests/test_media_detail.py with shared fixtures: catalog_db (from conftest), media_db_path/media_db (file-backed DB), detail_seeded_db (media file 'test1' with full metadata including codec='h264', fps=30.0, resolution 1920x1080, gps, sha256_prefix, audio_channels=2, metadata_json with extra fields, plus: transcript with segments_json containing 3 segments with timestamps/speakers, detections across 3 frames with objects like 'person'/'car'/'dog', 2 faces on separate frames assigned to cluster_id=1, face_cluster with label='Alice', 2 audio events with events_json containing classified events, 3 clip_embeddings, and a second media 'test2' with no analysis data), detail_app, detail_client.

steps.description: Write failing tests for CatalogDB.get_media_detail(media_id) in tests/test_media_detail.py: (1) returns None for non-existent media_id; (2) returns dict with 'media' key containing all media_files columns for existing media; (3) includes 'transcript' key with parsed segments list and language; (4) includes 'detections' key with list of detection rows; (5) includes 'faces' key with list of face rows; (6) includes 'audio_events' key with list of audio event rows; (7) includes 'embeddings' key with count of embeddings; (8) returns empty lists/None for media with no analysis data (test2).

Implement CatalogDB.get_media_detail(media_id) in autopilot/db.py. Returns None if media_id not found. Otherwise returns dict with keys: 'media' (from get_media), 'transcript' (from get_transcript, or None), 'detections' (from get_detections_for_media), 'faces' (from get_faces_for_media), 'audio_events' (from get_audio_events_for_media), 'embeddings' (from get_embeddings_for_media), 'embedding_count' (len of embeddings list), 'face_clusters' (dict mapping cluster_id to cluster info).

Write failing tests for GET /api/media/{media_id} JSON endpoint: (1) returns 404 for non-existent media_id; (2) returns 200 with JSON containing 'media', 'transcript', 'detections', 'faces', 'audio_events', 'embeddings' keys for existing media; (3) media section has all expected fields (codec, fps, resolution, etc.).
Implement GET /api/media/{media_id} route in media.py. Uses _get_db(request), calls db.get_media_detail(media_id), returns 404 HTTPException if None, otherwise returns the detail dict as JSON.

Write failing tests for GET /api/media/{media_id}/transcript: (1) returns 404 for missing media; (2) returns 200 with parsed segments list and language for media with transcript; (3) each segment has 'start', 'end', 'text', 'speaker' fields. And GET /api/media/{media_id}/detections: (1) returns 404 for missing media; (2) returns 200 with detection summary including 'total_detections' count, 'classes' dict with per-class counts, 'frame_count' (number of frames with detections).

Implement GET /api/media/{media_id}/transcript and GET /api/media/{media_id}/detections routes. Transcript endpoint parses segments_json into list of segment dicts. Detections endpoint aggregates across all frames: parses each frame's detections_json, counts total detections, builds per-class counts, and reports frame count.

Write failing tests for GET /media/{media_id} HTML detail page: (1) returns 404 for non-existent media_id; (2) returns 200 with text/html for existing media; (3) contains tab navigation with labels 'Metadata', 'Transcript', 'Detections', 'Faces', 'Audio Events', 'Embeddings'; (4) extends base.html (contains 'Autopilot Video' nav); (5) metadata tab content rendered by default (shows codec, resolution, duration); (6) tabs have hx-get attributes pointing to /media/{id}/tab/{name}.

Implement GET /media/{media_id} route and create templates/media/detail.html extending base.html. Tab navigation bar with HTMX hx-get attributes. Default active tab is metadata. Create partials/tab_metadata.html showing: codec, FPS, resolution (WxH), audio channels, duration (formatted), file path, SHA-256 prefix, dedup status, GPS coordinates, created_at timestamp, and parsed metadata_json fields.

Write failing tests for HTMX tab endpoints GET /media/{media_id}/tab/{tab_name}: (1) transcript tab returns HTML with timestamped lines like '[00:00:10]' and speaker labels; (2) transcript tab shows language indicator; (3) detections tab returns HTML with object class names and counts; (4) detections tab shows total detection count and frame coverage.

Implement HTMX tab endpoint GET /media/{media_id}/tab/{tab_name} in media.py that dispatches to tab-specific rendering logic. Create partials/tab_transcript.html (scrollable, time-stamped '[HH:MM:SS] Speaker: text' lines with language indicator) and partials/tab_detections.html (summary with top classes, total count, frame coverage, expandable grouped list by object class).

Write failing tests for remaining HTMX tab partials: (1) faces tab shows cluster grouping with cluster label 'Alice' and face count; (2) audio events tab shows timeline entries with event class names; (3) embeddings tab shows 'X of Y frames sampled (Z%)' format.

Create partials/tab_faces.html (grid grouped by cluster_id, showing cluster label from face_clusters table, count of appearances per cluster), partials/tab_audio_events.html (timeline-style list with parsed events_json, dominant event per time window), and partials/tab_embeddings.html (embedding count vs estimated total frames as 'X of Y frames sampled (Z%)').
Write failing test: media_row.html partial wraps the filename in a link to /media/{item.id} detail page.
Update partials/media_row.html to wrap the filename text in an <a href='/media/{{ item.id }}'> link styled with hover effect.

Write integration test: insert media with ALL analysis types (transcript, detections, faces with clusters, audio events, embeddings, captions), verify: detail page loads with 200, all tab endpoints return 200 with proper content, all 3 JSON API endpoints return correct structured data, edge case of media with no analysis returns empty/null sections gracefully.

Final polish: handle edge cases (empty JSON arrays, null optional fields, zero embeddings yielding 0% coverage, missing face cluster records). Ensure all tests pass end-to-end. Verify templates render gracefully with missing data.

design_decisions.decision: HTMX tab loading via dedicated /media/{id}/tab/{name} endpoint rather than embedding all tabs in initial page load
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the HTMX pattern already used in /api/media for partial swaps. Lazy-loads tab content on click, reducing initial page weight. Each tab is a separate Jinja2 partial for clean separation. The metadata tab is inlined in the initial render since it's the default.

design_decisions.decision: Add get_media_detail() to CatalogDB as a convenience aggregator rather than making multiple DB calls in route handlers
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps route handlers thin (matching existing pattern in media.py). Single method aggregates all related data, reusable by both the HTML detail page and the JSON API endpoint. Avoids duplicating query logic across 3+ route handlers.

design_decisions.decision: Parse JSON (detections_json, segments_json, events_json) in Python route handler, not in Jinja2 templates
design_decisions.rationale: Templates should receive pre-processed data. JSON parsing in Jinja2 is fragile and untestable. Route handlers parse JSON and pass structured Python dicts/lists to templates.

design_decisions.decision: Use 404 HTTPException for missing media_id across all endpoints
design_decisions.rationale: Standard REST pattern. Both JSON API and HTML endpoints return 404 when media_id doesn't exist. Consistent with typical FastAPI patterns.

design_decisions.decision: Estimate total frames from FPS * duration for embedding coverage calculation
design_decisions.rationale: CatalogDB stores fps and duration_seconds on media_files. Total frames = fps * duration gives approximate total. Avoids needing actual frame count metadata that doesn't exist in the schema.

design_decisions.decision: Detection summary aggregates by parsing detections_json across all frames in Python
design_decisions.rationale: Each frame's detections_json contains a list of detection dicts. Aggregating in Python (iterating all frames, counting classes) is simpler and more flexible than doing it in SQL with JSON functions. Frame counts are typically modest (hundreds, not millions).

design_decisions.decision: Single tab dispatch endpoint /media/{id}/tab/{name} rather than per-tab endpoints
design_decisions.rationale: Reduces route proliferation. One route with a path parameter dispatches to the right template. Invalid tab names return 404. Keeps the router clean.

reuse.what: CatalogDB per-table query methods: get_media(), get_transcript(), get_detections_for_media(), get_faces_for_media(), get_audio_events_for_media(), get_embeddings_for_media(), get_face_clusters(), get_face_cluster_by_id()
reuse.how: All needed DB query methods already exist. get_media_detail() composes them. No new SQL needed for individual table queries.
Reuse _get_db() for obtaining CatalogDB from request.app.state.db_path. Reuse _format_duration() for timestamp formatting in transcript tab.

reuse.what: base.html template with Tailwind dark theme, HTMX CDN, block structure
reuse.how: detail.html extends base.html exactly like list.html does via {% extends 'base.html' %}, filling {% block title %}, {% block content %}, {% block scripts %}.

reuse.what: Test fixture pattern: tmp_path DB -> CatalogDB -> seed -> create_app -> TestClient
reuse.how: Replicate the media_db_path/media_db/seeded_db/media_app/media_client fixture chain. Extend seeding to include all analysis types.
Use the shared catalog_db fixture for DB-level unit tests of get_media_detail().
Use TemplateResponse for full page renders and get_template().render() for HTMX partial responses, matching the existing dual-mode pattern.

reuse.what: CatalogDB insert/upsert methods for test seeding
reuse.how: Use insert_media(), upsert_transcript(), batch_insert_detections(), batch_insert_faces(), insert_face_cluster(), batch_insert_audio_events(), batch_insert_embeddings() to seed test data.
